text:
BOTTOM LINE — Semis-led, rates dragged, energy faded.  
S&P 500 +0.83%, but SOX +5.45% set the tape after the Fed hold; QQQs outperformed on hardware momentum, while bonds slipped and energy gave back gains. The day was a narrow tech-led rally driven by a fully anticipated neutral Fed and a sharp semis rotation, with zero follow-through in cyclicals or defensives.

INDICES — VIX 17.1, flat vs MA20. Rank: SOX +5.45%, QQQ +1.85%, IWM +1.45%, SPY +0.83%, DIA +0.69%. Large > small by +0.40pp. Silver +4.12% led cross-asset; TLT -0.27% flat.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.8x (low, price up = low-conviction rally), QQQ vol_ratio 2.1x (elevated, price up = accumulation). SOXX and IJR saw elevated volume; semis are gaining real interest, while SPY volume decline confirms the rally is narrow.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +2.89%, XLE Energy +0.97%, XLV Healthcare +0.63%, XLI Industrials +0.30%, XLY Discretionary +0.23%, XLB Materials +0.14%, XLF Financials +0.12%, XLU Utilities -0.04%, XLRE Real Estate -0.07%, XLC Comms -0.69%, XLP Staples -0.94%. Top Industries: DRAM +10.91%, SOXX +5.45%; Bottom: CIBR -1.32%, KWEB -1.53%. Money-in tech/semis, money-out comms/staple defensives — label: AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — QQQ PCR_vol 2.16 (extreme put demand), SPY PCR_vol 1.23 (elevated protection). IV skew positive for both — fear premium intact. QQQ at 733 vs GEX flip 728, near dealer hedging zone; VIX at 17.1 just above 16.9 MA20 — mild elevation.

CROSS-ASSET — Silver +4.12% diverging from defensives (TLT -0.27%, LQD -0.28%) — commodity inflation play vs bond bearishness post-Fed.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Greed 65.4; top movers: SOXX +5.45% (semis), GXC +0.07% (unusual vol), DHR -10.99% (medtech distribution) — DHR vol spike (7x) signals heavy distribution in medtech. Watch: SOXX (sector leader near 20d lines), QQQE (vol spike +1.17% — accumulation in equal-weight tech), and GLD (+1.96% gold call skew low at 0.50 PCR — bullish signal divergence from bond selloff).
